claims.statement: Schema Authority Guard test (unchanged from V4/V5).
Legacy schema purge migration script (unchanged from V4/V5).
Ghost Closure Detector test (unchanged from V4/V5).
Action 1: Registry B default schema_dir resolves to canonical helen_os/schemas/ (unchanged from V5).
Action 4a: helen_os/test_skill_promotion_requires_receipts.py migrated to canonical path + constitutional fixture (unchanged from V5).
Action 4b: helen_os/test_failure_bridge_only_accepts_typed_failures.py migrated to canonical path + constitutional fixture (unchanged from V5).

Defensive enrichment lifted from legacy: FAILURE_REPORT_V1.failure_class is now an 8-value enum (SCHEMA_MISMATCH, ASSERTION_FAILURE, TOOL_ERROR, TIMEOUT, RESOURCE_EXHAUSTION, RECEIPT_REJECTION, NONDETERMINISM, UNKNOWN). Prevents typoed failure classes from entering evolution.

Defensive enrichment lifted from legacy: SKILL_PROMOTION_DECISION_V1.reason_code is now a 9-value enum (EVAL_PASS, TRANSFER_POSITIVE, CAPABILITY_CONSISTENT, EVAL_FAIL, TRANSFER_NEGATIVE, CAPABILITY_DRIFT, RECEIPT_MISSING, ROLLBACK_TRIGGERED, DOCTRINE_CONFLICT). Bounds governance reason-code vocabulary.

Defensive enrichment candidate (receipts.minItems=1 on SKILL_PROMOTION_PACKET_V1) was applied then REVERTED after broader test audit exposed a regression: reducer Gate 2 (ERR_RECEIPT_MISSING) became unreachable because the schema layer preempted it. Decision: receipt-presence is a governance-semantic concern, enforced at the reducer (see helen_os/governance/skill_promotion_reducer.py Gate 2). Schema enforces shape, not business rules. Current hash matches post-revert state (no minItems constraint).

Action 3 executed: Deleted 4 overlapping root duplicates (execution_envelope_v1.json, failure_report_v1.json, skill_promotion_decision_v1.json, skill_promotion_packet_v1.json) plus 3 dead naming variants (skill_promotion_decision_v1.schema.json, skill_promotion_case_v1.schema.json, skill_promotion_case_v1_schema.json). Delta audit (see V6 discussion) confirmed none of the deleted files were loaded by any main-branch consumer. 386/386 tests pass post-delete.
The legacy root-level schemas/ directory is purged (absent or empty of JSON schemas).
claims.note: Directory now contains 48 files (was 55 at V4, 55 at V5). Progress: 7 files removed via Action 3 + variant cleanup. Remaining 48 are orphan schemas: K8/HAL/kernel/builders/oracle_town categories. These require Actions 5-6 (migrate to helen_os/schemas/ or purge if dead) which is the next cycle per HAL decision SCHEMA-AUTHORITY-2026-04-16 (cum_hash 70c69307bce0). When this flips to PASS, a V7 CLOSED receipt issues.

note: V6 extends V5 with: (a) 2 schema enrichments applied (C8: failure_class enum, C9: reason_code enum); (b) 1 enrichment attempted-then-reverted with documented reason (C10: minItems on receipts violated reducer gate layering); (c) Action 3 complete — 4 overlapping duplicates + 3 dead variants deleted (C11), 55→48 files in legacy dir. Seam still BLOCKED on C12: 48 orphan schemas need migration or purge (Actions 5-6). Test suite: 386/386 pass against canonical authority; no consumer was reading from legacy root. Divergence from parallel session at ~/Documents/GitHub/helen_os_v1/: parallel session applied all 3 enrichments without broader-suite audit; this session caught the minItems regression in helen_os/tests/test_skill_promotion_requires_receipts.py::test_missing_receipts_rejected, which verified that Gate 2 of the reducer fires before the schema layer for this case. Recommendation: parallel session should re-apply revert to stay consistent.
